name: Vanya and Label
input: The only line of the input contains a single word s (1 ≤ |s| ≤ 100 000), consisting of digits, lowercase and uppercase English letters, characters '-' and '_'.
output: Print a single integer — the number of possible pairs of words, such that their bitwise AND is equal to string s modulo 109 + 7.
note:
For a detailed definition of bitwise AND we recommend to take a look in the corresponding article in Wikipedia.
In the first sample, there are 3 possible solutions:
•  z&_ = 61&63 = 61 = z
•  _&z = 63&61 = 61 = z
•  z&z = 61&61 = 61 = z
